reasoning: Given the current observation, we have x = v*t + x0. To find the initial position x0, we can manipulate the equation by setting t and v as constants and solving for x0. To do this, we will first set v as a constant and take multiple measurements of x at different t values. This will allow us to find the slope of the linear relationship between x and t, which is v. Once we have v, we can substitute it back into the original equation and solve for x0. Therefore, we will first measure x at different t values.
Given the linear relationship between x and t, we can take another measurement to find the slope more accurately. We will increase t to get a new x value.

Given the linear relationship between x and t, we can now find the slope (v) by calculating the change in x (Δx) divided by the change in t (Δt). Substituting v back into the original equation (x = v*t + x0), we can solve for x0.
